options.text: So just to confirm—I can borrow 10 books, access JSTOR with my student login, and book rooms online?
Can I borrow 10 books, use JSTOR, and book rooms?
I think I can borrow 10 books and use JSTOR, right?

content.explanation: 正确答案使用了'so just to confirm'这一地道的确认句式，完整涵盖三项服务且语气礼貌，符合道别前确认信息的场景。选项2虽语法正确，但缺乏确认语气，显得像初次询问而非确认。选项3表达不完整且语气不确定，未能涵盖所有要点。选项4是单纯告别，未进行信息确认，不符合场景需求。
content.scenarioHint: 用户在图书馆办理完手续后，想确认自己能借阅的书籍数量、JSTOR访问权限和在线预约房间的功能是否都已开通，准备礼貌告别。

options.text: Yes, absolutely. No worries at all. Enjoy your week!
I have no idea, call customer service.
You should have thought of that earlier.
I guess so, but don't blame me if something goes wrong.

content.explanation: 正确答案 'Yes, absolutely. No worries at all. Enjoy your week!' 是标准的确认+安抚+道别表达，自然且专业。干扰项 'I have no idea...' 推卸责任；'You should have thought...' 指责对方；'I guess so...' 表达犹豫，削弱信任，都不符合道别确认场景的积极、确定、礼貌要求。
content.speakerText: Just to double-check—I can reschedule my appointment until next week, and I won’t be charged any fee?
content.speakerTextCn: 我再确认一下——我可以把预约改到下周，而且不会被收费，对吗？
